description: Demo of the individual-relationship system. Five NPCs whose behaviour deliberately contradicts their faction colours: two bandits who are individual enemies (Karn ↔ Dell, −100) fight each other despite sharing a faction; a bandit (Mott) and a Fortune-Guard warden (Bryn) are individual friends (+90) despite the bandits ↔ fortune_guards −60 feud, so they stand together; and a bandit (Wess) owes the player a life-debt (+80, seeded by trigger) so he reads friendly and won't fight the party. The encounter neutralises only bandits ↔ party (so combat doesn't auto-start and the player can observe), leaving every other faction line intact — the off-camera world tick then plays the brawl out: faction-mates killing each other, a guard fighting beside an outlaw.
customIntroduction: Mile Nine, and a crew coming apart at the seams. Five figures stand on the open road, and not one of them is where the colours on their cloaks would put them. Two bandits circle each other with bared steel — Karn and Dell, who rode together three winters until one sold the other out. A Fortune-Guard warden stands easy beside a third bandit, Mott, as though the bounty on his head were someone else's paperwork. And off to one side a fourth outlaw, Wess, lifts an empty hand to you — something like greeting.
customContext: DEMO ENCOUNTER — individual relationships override faction defaults. Relationship facts to keep consistent if the player talks to anyone: (1) Karn and Dell are bandits of the same crew but mortal personal enemies — Dell sold the crew's hideout to a Fortune-Guard creditor; they will try to kill each other. (2) Mott is a bandit but loyal to Warden Bryn (a Fortune Guard) who once saved his life; they fight side by side though their factions are enemies. (3) Wess is a bandit who owes the PLAYER a life-debt from the Blackgorge road and will not raise a blade against the player, whatever his crew does. The bandits are NOT hostile to the player by default here (the player can watch, approach, or intervene). Narrate the brawl as faction loyalty unravelling into personal loyalty.
objective: Watch who draws on whom — the cloak colours won't tell you. (Demo: individual relationships overriding faction lines.)

then.text: Watch who draws on whom — the cloak colours won't tell you.

then.message: Karn and Dell — same crew, same colours — turn their blades on each other. A Fortune-Guard warden stands at a bandit's shoulder. And Wess lowers his guard the moment he sees your face.
Wess keeps his blade lowered as you near. "Not you," he says. "Never you — I remember Blackgorge."
